texto: When works of art are presented like rare butterflies on the walls, they're decontextualized. We admire their beauty, and I have nothing against that, per se. But there is more to art than that.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hans Haacke: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a trabalho. O trecho “When works of art are presented like rare butterflies on the walls, they're decontextualized. We …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que trabalho, art, admire pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e admire.

frase_original: When works of art are presented like rare butterflies on the walls, they're decontextualized. We admire their beauty, and I have nothing against that, per se. But there is more to art than that.

texto: Museums are managers of consciousness. They give us an interpretation of history, of how to view the world and locate ourselves in it. They are, if you want to put it in positive terms, great educational institutions. If you want to put it in negative terms, they are propaganda machines.
explicacao: Citação de Hans Haacke sobre temas ligados a educacao. “Museums are managers of consciousness. They give us an interpretation of history, of how to view …” articula educacao, positive, great em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como great e world.

frase_original: Museums are managers of consciousness. They give us an interpretation of history, of how to view the world and locate ourselves in it. They are, if you want to put it in positive terms, great educational institutions. If you want to put it in negative terms, they are propaganda machines.
